channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: GOP sues over timing of special election crucial to Minnesota House power balance
item.description: A lawsuit challenges a decision by Gov. Tim Walz to schedule a Jan. 28 special election after a November winner was deemed ineligible to take office in the House on Jan. 14.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: The Latest: The legacy of Jan. 6 looms over election certification
item.description: Congress has gathered to certify President-elect Donald Trump’s election under the tightest national security level possible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump files motion to stay 'unlawful sentencing' in New York case
item.description: President-elect Trump's legal team filed a motion to stay the Jan. 10 sentencing in the New York v. Trump case that found him guilty last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Muslim, Arab voters face uncertain political future under Trump
item.description: Muslim and Arab American voters in the U.S. face an uncertain political future as President-elect Trump prepares to take office later this month.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ considers charging 200 more people 4 years after Jan. 6 Capitol attack
item.description: The Justice Department estimates up to 200 people are yet to be charged for their alleged involvement in the Jan. 6, 2021 riot at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nippon, U.S. Steel file suit after Biden administration blocks $15 billion deal
item.description: Nippon Steel and U.S. Steel are filing a federal lawsuit challenging the Biden administration’s decision to block a proposed nearly $15 billion deal for Nippon to acquire Pittsburgh-based U.S. Steel.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden blocks some new offshore drilling ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: Biden has announced that he’ll block new drilling off the entire East Coast, as well as California, Oregon and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds of Capitol riot prosecutions are in limbo as a DC court awaits Trump's White House return
item.description: Former Proud Boys extremist group leader Enrique Tarrio is urging President-elect Donald Trump to pardon him over the Jan. 6 insurrection as Trump’s return to power has thrown into question the future of the Jan. 6 cases.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer And Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden is traveling to New Orleans following the French Quarter attack that killed 14 and injured 30
item.description: President Joe Biden is heading to New Orleans as the city reels from the deadly New Year’s attack in which an Army veteran plowed a truck into revelers, killing 14 and injuring 30 more.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: As he prepares to leave office, Biden urges incoming Democratic lawmakers to reach across the aisle
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday called on incoming Democratic lawmakers who will govern in the minority to always be mindful of other people’s perspectives — even when they may be wrong.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who is Giorgia Meloni? Trump hosts Italian PM at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is firebrand politician who is likely Trump's closest ideological ally in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican Mike Johnson reelected House speaker in dramatic floor vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Mike Johnson narrowly won reelection Friday as House speaker on a first ballot, overcoming hard-right GOP holdouts in a tense standoff and buoyed by a nod of support from President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reminders of Jan. 6 attack fade in the Capitol as Trump retakes power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Inside the Capitol, reminders of the violence are increasingly hard to find. Scars on the walls have been repaired. Windows and doors broken by the rioters have been replaced. And there is no plaque, display or remembrance of any kind.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Johnson faces tough road on Trump agenda after dramatic Speaker vote
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) kept his gavel amid a threat from the right flank of the House GOP, but managing the House GOP is not going to get any easier from here.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money criminal case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but indicated he wouldn't be jailed.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former FBI agent: Transparency could stop conspiracy theorists
item.description: Gallagher said that while misinformation is "always a danger" in national events like these, "the FBI deals in evidence. Conspiracy theorists don't.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Biden awards the Medal of Honor and Medal of Valor to military heroes and first responders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — On Feb. 15, 1951, Army Pvt. Bruno R. Orig was returning from a mission when he found his fellow soldiers under attack in what's now known as the Battle of Chipyong-ni.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden blocks $14 billion acquisition of US Steel by Japan's Nippon Steel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden has blocked the nearly $15 billion proposed deal for Nippon Steel of Japan to purchase Pittsburgh-based U.S. Steel — affirming his earlier vow on the presidential campaign trail to prevent the acquisition of Steeltown USA’s most storied steel company.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, JOSH BOAK and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: With Board Chair Nora Vargas ending her term, here's who's vying to replace her
item.description: As of Friday, there are four people who have shown interest in running to replace Chair Nora Vargas on the San Diego County Board of Supervisors at the end of her term.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Nathan Fletcher's accuser dismisses sexual assault allegations from lawsuit
item.description: The former Metropolitan Transit System employee who accused Nathan Fletcher of sexual misconduct in a bombshell lawsuit that propelled his resignation from office has walked back some of her most serious allegations in new court filings.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Republican Mike Johnson reelected House speaker vote after dramatic floor vote
item.description: Republican Mike Johnson has won re-election to House speakership on first-round voting. He pushed past GOP holdout and got a boost from President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Live updates: Mike Johnson wins Speakership on first ballot after 2 'no' votes flip
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) succeeded in keeping his gavel for the 119th Congress, winning in the House's Speakership vote in one round Friday. Johnson, who had a one-vote margin of error, won 218-215 over Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.). Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: Chip Roy bid for Rules chair emerges as key issue ahead of Speaker vote
item.description: Rep. Chip Roy’s (R-Texas) bid to be chair of the House Rules Committee is emerging as a key issue for those withholding support for Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) ahead of Friday afternoon’s Speaker election. Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-Colo.), who has withheld support for Johnson, told former Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson battles for the Speakership: 5 things to watch
item.description: Friday’s Speaker vote is shaping up to be a nail-biter as Rep. Mike Johnson (R-La.), the current House leader, is scrambling to keep the gavel for the next two years in the face of conservative critics furious with his leadership style.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to award Medal of Honor to Korean and Vietnam War veterans
item.description: The Medal of Honor, the United States’ highest military decoration, is awarded to service members who display exceptional valor in combat.
item.dc:creator: Lexie Schapitl, MPR News

item.title: FBI checks, ethics paperwork threaten to slow down Trump confirmations
item.description: Republican sources on Capitol Hill warn that President-elect Trump’s nominees may be delayed because of paperwork holdups and the slow start of FBI background checks, creating obstacles for the speedy confirmation of Trump’s national security team.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says he will block US Steel sale to Nippon Steel
item.description: President Biden announced Friday he would block the sale of U.S. Steel to Japanese-owned Nippon Steel following a yearlong review of the potential acquisition. “We need major U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Conservatives seek commitments as Johnson scrambles for Speaker votes
item.description: Roughly a dozen hard-line House conservatives are demanding a host of amorphous commitments from Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) to win their support Friday, throwing his leadership tenure into uncertainty less than 24 hours before the vote.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota prosecutor wants to keep word ‘senator’ out of Nicole Mitchell trial
item.description: A high-profile trial is due to start this month over a state lawmaker’s burglary arrest. A prosecutor wants to keep Nicole Mitchell’s “senator” title away from the jury.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Imperial Beach mayor vying for San Diego County Supervisor
item.description: After San Diego County Board of Supervisors Chair Nora Vargas announced her decision to vacate her position at the end of her current term, three people have announced their desire to sit in her place.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: 5 ways Jimmy Carter’s presidency touched Minnesota
item.description: From his vice presidential pick, to protecting the Boundary Waters and more, much of former President Jimmy Carter’s legacy passes through Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Megan Burks, Lukas Levin, and Cathy Wurzer, MPR News

item.title: How Minnesota’s lawmakers in D.C. will fit in to a second Trump presidency
item.description: Some members of Minnesota’s congressional delegation will be taking on new roles as Trump’s second presidency begins. Republicans will have a majority in the House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Brian Bakst, and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: 2024 in politics: A memorable year in the Minnesota Legislature
item.description: In the final days of 2024, Minnesota Now is looking back at some of the year’s most significant moments in politics. Members of the MPR News politics team joined the program to reflect on the 2024 legislative session and the year ahead.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Brian Bakst, Dana Ferguson, and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: 2024 in politics: Gov. Tim Walz’s rise to the national stage and the VP nomination
item.description: In the final days of 2024, Minnesota Now is looking back at some of the year’s most significant moments in politics. At the top of the list is Gov. Tim Walz’s rise to become the Democratic vice presidential nominee alongside Kamala Harris.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Brian Bakst, Aleesa Kuznetsov, and Ellie Roth, MPR News
